reason: Verified that the QA agent can use the GitHub tool to fetch and display the authenticated GitHub username through the UI, including permission flows.
steps: Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — page loaded successfully showing the Super Agent application with sidebar containing multiple agents.
Found and clicked the "QA-20260424-110523-z1qj" agent in the sidebar — agent details page loaded, showing agent status as "idle".
Verified agent status is "idle" — status indicator at top right shows "idle", meeting the requirement of "running" or "idle".
Clicked the message input field — input field became active and ready for text entry.
Typed message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — message was successfully entered into the input field.
Clicked Send button — message was submitted, a new session "GitHub Identity Verification Request" was created, and agent status changed to "working".

Waited 3 seconds and checked for access request card — a "Request Connected Account GitHub" card appeared asking to "Allow access to GitHub to look up the authenticated username?" with GitHub already selected.
Clicked "Allow Access (1)" button — GitHub account access was granted, agent status returned to "working".
Waited 30 seconds for additional processing — an "API Request Review" card appeared asking to allow "GET /user" from GitHub.
Clicked "Allow" button on API request review card — a permission dialog appeared with multiple options.
Clicked "Allow Once" button — permission was granted, agent status returned to "working".
Waited 30 seconds for response completion — agent status changed to "idle" and the response message appeared: "Your GitHub username is yiw190."
Took screenshot of final response — screenshot captured showing the complete conversation including the GitHub username "yiw190" displayed in the response message.
